content.claim: Entrance examinations to 6- and 8-year gimnáziums are centrally organized, creating a legal tension between a centrally administered exam gate and a statutory free-choice regime guaranteed for all students by Act CXC.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The scope and content of central exam criteria are not detailed
No data provided on how the free-choice guarantee is operationalized in practice
The extent to which parental choice is constrained by exam-based filtering is not quantified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
